title: Submit content for editorial review
benefit: an editor can approve content before publication
description: As a content author, I want to submit a saved draft for editorial review so that an editor can check it before it is published.
acceptance_criteria: Given a saved draft exists, when the author submits it for review, then the workflow status changes to Submitted for review.
Given the draft has no title, when the author submits it for review, then the system prevents submission and displays a validation message.
Given the draft has no body, when the author submits it for review, then the system prevents submission and displays a validation message.
business_rules: Only saved drafts can be submitted for review.
A draft must have a title before review submission.
A draft must have body content before review submission.
edge_cases: Draft title contains leading or trailing spaces.
security_considerations: The author should only submit drafts they own.
The workflow should not expose drafts belonging to other users.
testability_notes: The workflow status should be visible or queryable.
Role and ownership assumptions should be explicit.

title: Publish approved content
benefit: approved content becomes available to the intended audience
description: As an editor, I want to publish content that has been submitted for review so that approved content can be made available.
acceptance_criteria: Given content is submitted for review, when an editor publishes it, then the workflow status changes to Published.
Given content is still in draft status, when an editor attempts to publish it, then the system prevents publication.
Given the user is an author, when they attempt to publish content, then the publish action is unavailable.
business_rules: Content must be in Submitted for review status before publication.
Published content must have a title and body.
edge_cases: Editor attempts to publish content immediately after status change.
Content is modified while under review.
negative_scenarios: Editor attempts to publish draft content.
Editor attempts to publish content with missing required fields.
security_considerations: Role-based access must prevent unauthorised publishing.
The system should not allow users to bypass review state transitions.
testability_notes: Publish button state should be testable.
Workflow status should be verifiable after publication.
Role-based permission behaviour should be deterministic.

title: Render content preview safely
benefit: content can be reviewed before it enters the publishing workflow
description: As a content author, I want to preview my content safely before submitting it for review so that I can check the final presentation.
acceptance_criteria: Given the author enters normal text, when the preview is displayed, then the text appears in the preview panel.
Given the author enters script-like content, when the preview is displayed, then it is rendered as text and not executed.
Given content is empty, when preview is displayed, then the system shows an empty-state message.
business_rules: Preview should not execute user-provided scripts.
Preview should represent the saved draft content.
Preview should not expose unpublished content to unauthorised users.
negative_scenarios: Preview content belonging to another user.
security_considerations: User-provided content must be rendered safely.
Preview must not create cross-site scripting risk.
testability_notes: The preview output should be visible to UI tests.
Safe rendering can be checked by comparing text content and HTML content.
Script execution can be checked through a browser-side flag.
